original_description: Implements WCAG 2.2 AA (+ AAA aspirational) accessibility for draft_v0 React 19 components built with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS v4. Use this skill whenever building or reviewing interactive components (buttons, forms, modals, menus, grids, trees, carousels), adding keyboard navigation, implementing ARIA roles/states/properties, managing focus, ensuring color or non-text contrast, supporting screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, TalkBack, Narrator), handling touch/pointer interactions, writing semantic HTML, running accessibility tests, or auditing for WCAG 2.2 compliance. Make sure to use this skill whenever the user mentions aria, wcag, a11y, accessible, keyboard nav, focus trap, screen reader, skip link, tab order, contrast ratio, or any WCAG success criterion -- even if they don't explicitly say 'accessibility'.
best_description: Implements WCAG 2.2 AA (+ AAA aspirational) accessibility for draft_v0 React 19 components built with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS v4. Use this skill whenever building or reviewing interactive components (buttons, forms, modals, menus, grids, trees, carousels), adding keyboard navigation, implementing ARIA roles/states/properties, managing focus, ensuring color or non-text contrast, supporting screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, TalkBack, Narrator), handling touch/pointer interactions, writing semantic HTML, running accessibility tests, or auditing for WCAG 2.2 compliance. Make sure to use this skill whenever the user mentions aria, wcag, a11y, accessible, keyboard nav, focus trap, screen reader, skip link, tab order, contrast ratio, or any WCAG success criterion -- even if they don't explicitly say 'accessibility'.
final_description: Use this skill when a UI component doesn't work right for keyboard-only users or screen reader users — tab can't reach something, arrow keys don't navigate a widget, focus disappears after closing a dialog, a screen reader isn't announcing content, or ARIA roles/states aren't set up correctly. Also use it for WCAG compliance work: axe-core violations, success criterion questions (like SC 1.3.5 autocomplete attributes), VPAT documentation, color contrast failures, or building accessible versions of comboboxes, data grids, menus, modals, or trees. Trigger even when the user never says "accessibility" — if they're describing a broken interaction that affects users who can't use a mouse, rely on a screen reader, or need keyboard navigation, this skill applies.

history.description: Implements WCAG 2.2 AA (+ AAA aspirational) accessibility for draft_v0 React 19 components built with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS v4. Use this skill whenever building or reviewing interactive components (buttons, forms, modals, menus, grids, trees, carousels), adding keyboard navigation, implementing ARIA roles/states/properties, managing focus, ensuring color or non-text contrast, supporting screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, TalkBack, Narrator), handling touch/pointer interactions, writing semantic HTML, running accessibility tests, or auditing for WCAG 2.2 compliance. Make sure to use this skill whenever the user mentions aria, wcag, a11y, accessible, keyboard nav, focus trap, screen reader, skip link, tab order, contrast ratio, or any WCAG success criterion -- even if they don't explicitly say 'accessibility'.

train_results.query: some of our users use keyboard only — no mouse. they're saying they can't reach the buttons inside our data table because tab just skips over the cells. data grid keyboard model help?
building a search autocomplete. screen readers aren't announcing the suggestions at all. it's just a plain input and ul. how do i make this a proper combobox?
running axe-core on our login page and it's flagging SC 1.3.5 — says our email and password fields are missing autocomplete attributes. what values should i use?
client is asking for a VPAT for our SaaS app. never done one before. what sections does it need and how do we fill out the wcag conformance tables?
my modal dialog is using FocusScope but when it closes focus just disappears instead of going back to the button that opened it. how do i fix the return focus?

hey so i'm building this dropdown menu with shadcn and when you tab to it and hit enter it opens but then you can't navigate the items with arrow keys. do i need to add keyboard support myself or does radix handle it?
what's the difference between useCallback and useMemo in react 19? when should i use which one?
want to add a tooltip to my icon buttons using shadcn Tooltip. it should show on hover and also when the button is focused with keyboard. is this already built in?
setting up playwright for e2e testing in my next.js app. how do i test that a form submits correctly and shows a success message?
i want to add a nice animated page transition in my react router app. what's the best framer motion pattern for this? something smooth that slides between routes
how do i add a loading spinner that shows while my API call is in flight? i'm using tanstack query. want it to replace the content area, not overlay it
i have a react component that re-renders too often. when i add a console.log inside i see it fires 6 times on a single button click. how do i debug this?

test_results.query: the focus ring only shows up sometimes on our buttons. looks like it triggers on mouse click too which our designer hates. how do i show it only for keyboard navigation in tailwind
our design team says we need to pass a WCAG 2.2 audit before launch. i don't know where to start — we have about 40 React components. what's the process?
i need to add aria-expanded and aria-haspopup to my nav toggle button but i'm not sure which values to use exactly and where to put them
my image carousel auto-plays every 4 seconds. a user filed a bug saying they get motion sick. also it's not keyboard accessible at all. two problems, need to fix both
my shadcn Dialog component doesn't close when i click outside. how do i override the default behavior to keep it open until the user explicitly clicks the X button?
need to style a button component using tailwind css v4. want it to have a hover effect, active state, and disabled state. what's the cleanest way to do this?
building a form with react hook form and zod. need validation that shows error messages below each field. what's the best way to wire up the schema?
how do i add dark mode support to my shadcn/ui app? i want to use the class strategy with a toggle button that saves the preference to localStorage

history.description: Use this skill for any accessibility, WCAG, or assistive technology question. Trigger on: keyboard-only navigation problems, focus management (focus traps, return focus, focus disappearing), screen reader issues (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), ARIA roles and live regions, autocomplete attributes and input purpose (SC 1.3.5), combobox/autocomplete announcements, data grid keyboard models, axe-core violations, VPAT documentation, color contrast, skip links, tab order, and any WCAG success criterion. Also trigger when the user's problem involves users who can't use a mouse, assistive technology not announcing content, interactive widgets missing keyboard support (arrow keys, roving tabindex), or compliance reporting for enterprise clients. Works with shadcn/ui, Radix UI, React 19, and Tailwind CSS v4.

history.description: Invoke this skill whenever a user's problem touches disability access, assistive technology, or inclusive design — even when they never say the word "accessibility". Covers: users who rely on keyboard only (no mouse), screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) not hearing announcements, focus going missing or to the wrong place after interactions, ARIA patterns for custom widgets (combobox, data grid, tree, toolbar), autocomplete attribute requirements, axe-core audit violations, WCAG success criteria questions, color contrast failures, skip links, VPAT/conformance reporting for enterprise clients, and making shadcn/Radix/React components meet WCAG 2.2 AA. Also trigger when the user asks why keyboard navigation feels broken, why a screen reader isn't announcing something, how to implement roving tabindex, or how to file a conformance report.
